# vibe 🌊

> vibe is a zsh plugin that transforms natural language into shell commands using AI. Type what you want in plain English, press Ctrl+G, and get an OS-aware command with inline explanations. It works with Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), Groq, OpenRouter, and more, and ships as a single zero-dependency compiled Go binary.

## Overview

- Natural language to shell commands: describe intent in plain English, press Ctrl+G to generate.
- OS-aware generation: automatically detects and targets macOS, Linux, or Windows.
- Fast: first query ~500ms-2s, cached responses ~5-10ms (100-400x faster).
- Multi-provider: hosted (openai, anthropic, groq, openrouter, deepseek, google-openai, mistral, cohere) and local (ollama, lmstudio, vllm) via gollm.
- Safe by default: commands are never executed automatically; review and edit before running.
- Inline explanations for every part of the generated command.
- Zero dependencies: single compiled binary, no Node.js or Python required.
- All configuration via environment variables in ~/.zshrc; all settings optional with sensible defaults.

## Docs

- [Documentation Home](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/): Overview and quick links to all guides.
- [Installation](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/installation/): Install via Homebrew (brew tap skymoore/tap; brew install --cask vibe-zsh) or other methods.
- [Configuration](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/configuration/): Configure your LLM provider via environment variables (VIBE_PROVIDER, VIBE_API_KEY, VIBE_MODEL, VIBE_API_URL).
- [Usage](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/usage/): How to use vibe, keybindings, and query history.
- [Troubleshooting](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/troubleshooting/): Common issues and solutions.
- [Configuration Reference](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/reference/): Complete environment variable reference.
- [API Reference](https://vibe-zsh.dev/docs/api/): Internal API and architecture for developers and contributors.

## Optional

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/skymoore/vibe-zsh): Source code, issues, and releases.
